contextBlurb: Texas established a foundational legal framework for digital assets in 2021 by amending its Uniform Commercial Code to formally define virtual currency and enable security interests in cryptocurrency. The Texas Department of Banking also issues ongoing guidance for virtual currency businesses operating in the state.

legislation.title: Texas Virtual Currency Bill — UCC Amendment
legislation.summary: Amends the Texas Uniform Commercial Code to formally define "virtual currency" and establish legal frameworks for owning, securing, and controlling digital assets. Enables security interests in cryptocurrency to be perfected, allowing crypto to be used as collateral and providing legal certainty for crypto-related businesses. Effective September 1, 2021.

legislation.title: Texas Department of Banking — Virtual Currency Guidance
legislation.summary: Ongoing regulatory guidance from the Texas Department of Banking on virtual currency businesses operating in the state, including licensing and consumer protection expectations.
